content.problemStatement:
There are 333 problems in a contest namely A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C respectively. Alice bets Bob that problem CCC is the hardest while Bob says that problem BBB will be the hardest.

You are given three integers SA,SB,SCS_A, S_B, S_CSA‚Äã,SB‚Äã,SC‚Äã which denotes the number of successful submissions of the problems A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C respectively. It is guaranteed that each problem has a different number of submissions. Determine who wins the bet.



If Alice wins the bet (i.e. problem CCC is the hardest), then output AliceAliceAlice.




If Bob wins the bet (i.e. problem BBB is the hardest), then output BobBobBob.




If no one wins the bet (i.e. problem AAA is the hardest), then output DrawDrawDraw.



**Note**: The hardest problem is the problem with the least number of successful submissions.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers SA,SB,SCS_A, S_B, S_CSA‚Äã,SB‚Äã,SC‚Äã, denoting the number of successful submissions of problems A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output the winner of the bet or print Draw in case no one wins the bet.
constraints: SA,SB,SCS_A, S_B, S_CSA‚Äã,SB‚Äã,SC‚Äã are all distinct.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:  Problem AAA turns out to be the hardest so no one wins the bet.
Test case 222:  Problem BBB turns out to be the hardest so Bob wins the bet.
Test case 333:  Problem CCC turns out to be the hardest so Alice wins the bet.
